book_summary: Leviticus has begun with chapters 1-7 complete in restored/source-audited draft and mystical companion layers. The opening offering manual is framed as ancient ritual grammar for drawing near after the tabernacle is filled with glory: burnt/whole offering, grain offering, peace/well-being offering, purification/sin offering, guilt/reparation offering, restitution, thanksgiving, vow/freewill offerings, priestly portions, and holy meal boundaries.

chapters.notes: YHWH calls from tent; burnt/whole offering from herd, flock, or birds; blood/life and altar approach.
Grain/minhah offering, oil, frankincense, memorial portion, priestly portion, salt of covenant, firstfruits.
Peace/well-being offering, herd/flock/goat, fat portions, blood/life prohibition.
Unintentional/hidden sin-misalignment, priest/congregation/leader/common person, atonement and forgiveness.
Specific guilt cases, confession, sliding-scale offerings, birds/flour access, holy-things reparation.
Fraud/theft restitution plus fifth, continual altar fire, ash service, priestly instructions.
Guilt offering, priestly portions, thanksgiving/vow/freewill peace offerings, fat/blood prohibitions, offering manual summary.

translation_notes: Leviticus continues Exodus: YHWH calls from the completed tent of meeting.
Korban/offering means something brought near; offerings are approach/nearness grammar, not crude appeasement.
Blood/life should be treated as ritual life-symbolism, not gore spectacle.
Clean/unclean language is ritual-state language, not body shame, moral disgust, disability stigma, or human worth ranking.
Minhah/grain offering and flour options show non-animal offering pathways and accessible repair for the poor.
Shelamim/peace-well-being offering should retain wholeness/communion dimensions.
Chatat should be flagged as sin/purification offering; the first pass renders sin/misalignment while noting purification function.
Asham/guilt-reparation offering includes concrete restitution and added fifth where harm/loss occurred.
Leaven is not universally negative: it is excluded from some altar offerings but included with thanksgiving peace offering in Leviticus 7.
Fat and blood/life prohibitions should not become body-shame; they reserve abundance/life to YHWH in ritual grammar.
Spiritual repair does not replace material restitution. Leviticus 6 requires return plus added fifth before/with offering.
Mystical companion layer remains interpretive and does not replace source-audited restored text.
source_witness_audit_flags: Leviticus/Vayiqra title and opening call from tent requires Torah continuity crosslink with Exodus 40.
Korban/nearness terminology requires lexical audit across offering texts.
Olah/burnt-whole/ascending offering terminology requires lexical audit.
Minhah/grain offering terminology requires lexical and socio-economic access audit.
Shelamim/peace-well-being offering requires lexical and meal-context audit.
Chatat as sin/purification offering requires deep lexical and ritual-function audit.
Clean/unclean terminology requires anti-shame and ritual-state metadata across Leviticus.
Leviticus 5 sliding-scale offerings require poverty/accessibility audit.
Leviticus 6 restitution plus fifth requires justice/repair crosslink metadata.
Leviticus 7 thanksgiving/vow/freewill offering timing requires ritual meal-boundary audit.
Fat/blood prohibitions require life-symbolism and anti-body-shame safeguards.
next_actions: Continue Leviticus with chapters 8-10: ordination of Aaron and sons, first priestly offerings, glory appearing, Nadab and Abihu, priestly boundaries, and grief/ritual tension.
